title: Hamo Blastwell
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image= |name=Hamo Blastwell |homeworld=A [[Hamo Blastwell's homeworld|small outpost planet]]{{StoryCite|story=The Farlander Papers|book=Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)}} |birth=c. [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]Though Blastwell's age is not stated in any source, in both ''[[The Farlander Papers]]'' and ''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'', Blastwell is described as only a few years older than [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]], who estimates himself to be about the same age as [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] when they meet just prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places Skywalker's birth in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] and the Battle of Yavin in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], indicating that Blastwell is in his early twenties at that time. Based on ''The New Essential Chronology'', [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] was a few years before 19 BBY. |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Dark blond]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] **[[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] **[[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]] **[[Gold Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Gold Squadron]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|After they plan what has to be done, you know who'll do it, don't ya?|Hamo Blastwell|''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]''}} '''Hamo Blastwell''' was a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who served in [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] on the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He served as the squadron's orientation [[Officer/Legends|officer]], helping to introduce new pilots to the squadron and assisting with their training. After participating in several battles, and playing an important role in helping to defeat [[Operation Strike Fear]], Blastwell was promoted to [[Commander/Legends|commander]]. Following his promotion he became heavily involved in mission planning and special missions, spending less time flying with the squadron. Blastwell flew in the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], in which he was believed to have been killed. However, he survived and went undercover in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] using the alias '''Trace'''. During his time with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Blastwell assisted in bringing a [[Sepan campaign|resolution]] to the [[Sepan Civil War]]. Following the defection of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Harkov]], Blastwell successfully infiltrated the [[Prophets of the Dark Side|Secret Order of the Emperor]]. He eventually came to serve under [[Demetrius Zaarin]], shortly before the [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral's]] coup against [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. Blastwell later attempted to steal the plans to the [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE/D Defender]] for the Alliance. Although he was unsuccessful, he was able to evade capture. ==Biography== ===Orientation officer=== {{Quote|You don't waste time around here, do you? I thought I'd have to cool my heels for days before I got a crack at anything like real training.''"
"''Hey, Keyan. Get this. The Alliance has no time to waste. No time at all.|Keyan Farlander and Hamo Blastwell|}} Born around [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] and originally from a small outpost [[Hamo Blastwell's homeworld|planet]], little was known of Hamo Blastwell's life before leaving his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] to join the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]—he did once admit to having met [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], a key figure in the Alliance who hailed from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]{{DB|character|leiaorganasolo|Solo, Princess Leia Organa}} [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], but was reluctant to discuss any details. Within a year of joining the Alliance, and still in his early twenties, Blastwell had attained the rank of [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] and the status of [[Ace/Legends|ace]] pilot. Serving as a wing leader in [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] aboard the Alliance [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']] in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' begins after the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] entered service with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], which ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' places in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' establishes that the [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|capture of Leia Organa]] occurs at the end of [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]{{'s}} second tour of duty. Organa's capture takes place on 35:3:3 under the [[Great ReSynchronization]] dating system, placing it at the beginning of [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] according to [[Template:YearMathGRS|the following explanation]]. Events in ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' before the end of the second tour of duty therefore take place in 1 BBY. Blastwell acted as liaison and orientation [[Officer/Legends|officer]] to new recruits, including [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]], whose similar background led to the two quickly becoming good friends. In this role, Blastwell would prepare recruits for the [[pilot proving grounds]], drilling them in every spare moment, until they could follow his every move without the need for verbal communication and he considered them ready to begin simulated combat training. [[File:Talon1.jpg|thumb|left|260px|Blastwell and Farlander fly X-wings during the attack on the ''Talon''.]] Blastwell had high hopes for Farlander early in his training, and so volunteered him for his first mission before the young pilot had completed combat training. Continuing in his role as mentor, Blastwell pulled Farlander from the simulators to get some real flight experience before the mission and selected the younger pilot to serve as his wingman. The [[Battle in the Turkana system|mission]], to retrieve information stored on the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|corvette]] [[Talon (Imperial corvette)|''Talon'']], saw Blastwell flying an [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] as [[Red Leader/Legends|Red Leader]] to assist the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B frigate]] [[Liberator (EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate)|''Liberator'']] in engaging [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighter]] escorts while [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]] from [[Gold Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Gold Squadron]] disabled the ''Talon'' for boarding.[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] Although Blastwell's [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2 unit]] sustained damage, leaving him unable to enter [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and forcing him to return to the ''Liberator'', the mission justified Blastwell's faith in Farlander as the recruit played a vital role in capturing the data. ===Operation Strike Fear=== The Rebels' recent successes against the Empire provoked an Imperial response—a massive offensive known as [[Operation Strike Fear]]. News of what the Empire was planning came to light during a [[Battle in the Dellalt system|raid]] on an Imperial convoy in which the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the corvette ''[[Bixby]]'' indicated his intention to defect. While [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron's]] Y-wings covered the defection of the ''Bixby'', Blastwell and Farlander were scrambled to destroy the convoy. Shortly after the capture of the ''Bixby'', Blastwell was promoted to captain. Blastwell would be heavily involved in the fight against Operation Strike Fear, taking part in several missions. With the Alliance running short of [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droids]], the Rebels mounted an [[Raid in the Rudrig system|operation]] to steal some R2 units from the [[BFF-1 bulk freighter/Legends|BFF-1 bulk freighter]] ''[[Ars Opus]]'', unaware that the Galactic Empire had modified them to take control of X-wings for the Empire to examine. While he was flying a mission with Blue Squadron, Blastwell's X-wing was one of three hijacked and taken to a rendezvous with the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Invincible (Strike Fear)|''Invincible'']]. [[File:Blastwell2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Blastwell's X-wing is rescued by a shuttle.]] The Rebels mounted a quick [[Mission to recover stolen X-wings|rescue mission]] to recover the stolen ships and their pilots before they could be delivered to the ''Invincible''. Tracking the X-wings to the ''Invincible''{{'s}} location, three Y-wings from Red Squadron disabled them, allowing [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttles]] to deliver new R2 units and the pilots to take control of their ships and fly them to the waiting [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] ''[[Maximus]]''. Though two of the Y-wings entered hyperspace as soon as their targets were disabled, Keyan Farlander, aware that his friend was in one of the X-wings, engaged [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] fighters launched from ''Invincible'' and protected the rescue of the pilots. The incident left Blastwell with a badly sprained wrist which prevented him from flying for a time. Though unable to fly, Blastwell remained involved with the campaign. In a private briefing with fellow Alliance officer [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[H. S'man|S'man]] three days after receiving his injury, Blastwell was charged with overseeing Farlander's preparations for a [[Rescue of Sullustan Tech Staff|mission]] to rescue prisoners of the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] species. The successful rescue helped gain support for the Alliance on Sullust. Blastwell returned to active duty as Farlander's flight leader as the two flew [[R-22 Spearhead starfighter|R-22 Spearheads]] in defense of a [[Diplomatic Summit at Sullust|diplomatic meeting]] between the Alliance and the Sullustans near [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]], the Sullustan species's home planet.{{DB|species|sullustan|Sullustan}}. After the arrival of the ''Invincible'', the two pilots held off Imperial fighters until the meeting was concluded and all ships fled into hyperspace. The ''Invincible''{{'s}} attack, however, turned out to be merely a diversion. While the meeting was taking place, [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] kidnapped the Sullustan leader. Blastwell was called into [[Rescue of Sian Tevv|action]] again, flying cover in a Gold Squadron X-wing while Farlander disabled the shuttle that bore the Sullustan to the ''Invincible'', and holding off Imperial fighters until a Rebel shuttle conducted a boarding operation and escaped into hyperspace. After the mission, Blastwell helped organize a party to celebrate Farlander's promotion to captain and was later one of the senior officers called to a briefing on the Alliance's plans to destroy the ''Invincible''. ===Search for the Death Star=== {{Quote|The engineers have decided that a single fighter can destroy this thing. Which sounds like a crock to me, but we're out of options.|Hamo Blastwell on the Death Star|}} The [[Destruction of the Invincible|destruction of the ''Invincible'']] put an end to Operation Strike Fear. However, Blastwell would have little time to celebrate as he was called off-ship for an assignment. The mission resulted in Blastwell receiving a promotion to commander, though after his return to the ''Independence'', he wouldn't discuss the circumstances of his promotion with the other pilots, describing it only as "a long story." [[File:Intrepid.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Alliance forces battle the ''Intrepid''.]] The Alliance had been investigating reports of an Imperial [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] for some time. The weapon turned out to be the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]]—a [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] with enough power to destroy a planet. After managing to acquire a set of [[Death Star plans/Legends|plans]] to the station, the Alliance planned to eliminate the Star Destroyer [[Intrepid (Imperial I-class)|''Intrepid'']] in order to prevent it from reporting their accomplishment. After a previous [[Battle of the Circarpous system (Galactic Civil War)|mission]] left the ''Intrepid'' stranded, Blastwell joined with Farlander, along with fellow pilots [[Samuel Raider]] and [[Ernek Marskan]], in a mission to destroy its escort of [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|Assault Gunboats]] and corvettes from [[Viper (corvette group)|Viper]] group. With the loss of the escort leaving the ship vulnerable, a subsequent assault was launched to destroy the ''Intrepid'' itself. The Death Star plans were to be transported to Alderaan by Princess Leia Organa. Blastwell flew protection for the [[Death Star plans delivery|transfer]] of the plans to her ship, [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']], and was called on again when the ship came under [[Assault on the Tantive IV|attack]] from the Star Destroyer ''[[Immortal/Legends|Immortal]]''. Although he helped see her safely to hyperspace, her escape was short-lived, as Imperial forces under [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] finally captured her near the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].{{Film|IV}} Blastwell's promotion to commander meant that he was more heavily involved in strategic planning and often privy to information before the other pilots. When the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Tuz]] brought the Alliance information on an Imperial repair facility, he delivered the information to Blastwell and S'man. A [[Attack on the Imperial repair dock|mission]] was promptly planned to destroy the facility. Between his mission-planning role and increasingly frequent involvement in special missions, which often took him off-ship for several days, Blastwell was spending less time flying with Red Squadron—though he officially remained a member of the squadron—and Farlander took over his role as orientation officer. ===Assault on ''Godar'' and Battle of Yavin=== {{Quote|The target area is only two meters wide. It's a small thermal exhaust port right below the main port. The shaft leads directly to the reactor system. A precise hit will start a chain reaction which should destroy the station.|Jan Dodonna in the briefing prior to the Battle of Yavin|}} One mission planned by Blastwell was an [[Capture of the Death Star design team|assault]] on the Imperial corvette ''[[Godar]]'', which was carrying members of the Death Star design team. After a week of planning, Blastwell selected Farlander to pilot a Y-wing to disable the vessel. Despite interference from the ''Immortal''{{'s}} starfighters, a Rebel team was able to capture the design team members, and Farlander destroyed the ''Godar'' before leaving. After another prolonged absence Blastwell returned to the ''Independence'' shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] to learn that Farlander's experimentation with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] had led him dangerously close to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and had alienated many of the other pilots. Blastwell waited for Farlander as he returned from a mission but, as he confronted his friend, Farlander collapsed due to a vision experienced through the Force. When he regained consciousness he refused to let Blastwell take him to a medic and asked that Blastwell not tell anybody what had happened. Though he did not really understand, Blastwell agreed and was true to his word. For his part, Farlander realized the error of his ways and resolved not to use the Force until he could control its effects. [[File:Battle_of_Yavin.png|thumb|right|260px|Alliance starfighters approach the Death Star.]] Blastwell was one of several pilots transferred to the [[Great Temple/Legends|Rebel Base]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] to assist in the upcoming battle against the Death Star in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' After attending the formal briefing by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]], Blastwell filled Farlander, who had been out on a preliminary mission, in on the plan. Though they would be flying in different groups for the upcoming battle, both were charged with holding off Imperial fighters while others attacked the Death Star itself. Though the Rebels suffered heavy losses, the Battle of Yavin was a resounding success for the Alliance as the newly recruited pilot [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] dealt a killing blow to the Imperial superweapon. Blastwell was believed to have been shot down in the battle and, though there were no witnesses and he was officially listed as missing, presumed killed. Blastwell's loss was particularly hard on Farlander but the young pilot was able to control his use of the Force and eventually became a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]].{{InsiderCite|57|Who's Who in the New Jedi Order}} ===Infiltrating the Empire=== Yet, unknown to those he served with, Blastwell survived the Battle of Yavin. Operating under the false identity Trace, Blastwell embarked on an undercover operation to infiltrate the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. By just after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]''[[TIE Fighter: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Blastwell had become a captain in the Imperial Navy and was one of the senior [[TIE pilot|pilots]] aboard the [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Protector (Victory I-class)|''Protector'']], under the command of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Harkov]]. The ''Protector'' was sent to [[Sepan campaign|intervene]] in the [[Sepan Civil War]] and Blastwell was present at Harkov's briefing on the upcoming campaign. Blastwell fought in at least one battle of the Sepan campaign, in which he helped rescue a convoy belonging to the Sepans' [[Ripoblus (people)|Ripoblus]] group from an enemy [[Dimok (people)|Dimok]] attack. After the mission Blastwell debriefed one of the ''Protector''{{'s}} new pilots, [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]]. Blastwell was impressed by Stele's piloting ability but noted that Stele had apparently exceeded orders by investigating the convoy, discovering Imperial weapons in the process. Blastwell instructed Stele to obey orders in the future and gave him a personal warning to avoid attracting the attention of Admiral Harkov. [[File:Cloaked_mage.jpg|thumb|left|210px|A member of the Secret Order whom Blastwell impersonated]] When Stele continued to exceed orders, discovering more illegal weapons in a subsequent mission, Blastwell became suspicious. When he saw Stele conversing with a [[Priest (Secret Order of the Emperor)|cloaked figure]], he became convinced that Stele was working with the mysterious [[Prophets of the Dark Side|Secret Order of the Emperor]] and saw an opportunity to learn more about the group. Disguising himself as a member of the order, he entered Stele's quarters one night and attempted to confirm his suspicions. However, when Stele saw through the deception, Blastwell told him to forget the incident had ever happened and left. Stele only saw Blastwell once more aboard the ''Protector'' when he noticed him watching from behind a bulkhead during Stele's next meeting with his liaison in the Secret Order. When Stele mentioned Blastwell's apparent interest in the Order he was told that they were already aware of it and would "take care of him." ===Further infiltration and Imperial promotions=== {{Quote|I will serve the Emperor, as I always have.|Maarek Stele, who Blastwell would convince to defect from the Empire|''[[TIE Fighter Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]''}} Soon, however, Blastwell was able to begin working for the Order himself. Soon after Admiral Harkov defected to the Rebel Alliance, the Secret Order made arrangements for Blastwell to flee the ''Protector''. Maintaining his cover as a loyal [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]], Blastwell took a [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptor]] and rendezvoused with loyal Imperial forces on the Star Destroyer [[Garrett (Star Destroyer)|''Garrett'']]. A few days after arriving he was reunited with Stele and attempted to mend fences, though Stele was initially wary. [[File:TIEDefender-FF73.png|thumb|right|240px|Blastwell attempted to steal the plans of the TIE/D Defender (pictured).]] In time, Blastwell was promoted to [[Major/Legends|major]] and sent to a research facility under the command of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin]]. There, he once again encountered Stele. Stele had been having doubts about the Empire and whether he was fighting for the right side—doubts which had only increased following the recent defection of his friend [[Carith]] to the Alliance. Blastwell had long thought that Stele was a decent man who simply hadn't come to see the evils committed by the Empire, and he decided that now was the time to attempt to draw the talented young pilot to the Alliance. After making Stele promise to keep their conversation confidential, Blastwell confided that he was an Alliance officer and attempted to make Stele see the truth about the Empire. However, though Stele was disillusioned about the Empire, he was not yet prepared to abandon it and the Imperial ideals he had once believed in. Though he refused to join the Alliance and told Blastwell to leave, he did agree to think about his position and to not turn Blastwell in. With Stele true to his word, Blastwell remained undercover in the Imperial Navy, continuing to serve under Grand Admiral Zaarin aboard his flagship, the Star Destroyer [[Glory (Imperial-class)|''Glory'']] and receiving a promotion to commander. Soon after Zaarin attempted a coup against [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], Blastwell attempted to steal the plans for the new [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE/D Defender]]. Blastwell's attempt to steal the plans was thwarted but he managed to escape the ''Glory''. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|You must deliver a telling blow to the Dimok aggressors, but allow some to escape to tell their superiors of the might and will of the Empire. Are there any questions?''"
"''How do we tell which of them to let escape? Draw straws?|Admiral Harkov and Blastwell during a briefing by the former|}} A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] with shaggy, [[Color/Legends|dark blond]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] [[Standard month/Legends|months]] overgrown, Blastwell's somewhat scruffy appearance and general demeanor seemed in contrast to his position as a military officer. This relaxed attitude extended to his command style, and Blastwell rarely insisted on being addressed by rank, generally preferring new recruits to call him by his first name. Despite his appearance Blastwell was a dedicated orientation officer. He was happy to share his greater combat experience with his recruits and would watch over their training carefully, reassuring them when they had doubts about their abilities and helping to build their confidence while keeping them grounded and reminding them that they had much to learn. Blastwell himself had great faith in his own ability to do whatever was asked of him. He was also a sociable person and enjoyed spending time with the other pilots in their ready room, often partaking in games of [[Horansi (game)/Legends|Horansi]] or discussing their combat experiences. His friends were important to him and he tried to be there to help them through difficult times—when Farlander was suspended from duty pending an inquest into the loss of two wingmen, Blastwell insisted on taking him for a drink to cheer him up. Later, his concern for Farlander made him confront the younger pilot when the rest of the squadron abandoned him. Blastwell's personality would later seem out of place in the more rigid Imperial Navy. A joke he made during Harkov's briefing on the Sepan campaign met with general laughter from the other pilots but Blastwell was called to Harkov after the conclusion of the briefing and disciplined. Harkov even went so far as to strike him for his lapse in discipline. ==Skills and abilities== Blastwell received successive promotions, which allowed his self-confidence to grow. ==Equipment== As an X-wing pilot lieutenant, Blastwell wore a [[Alliance military uniform/Legends|uniform]] typically only partially buttoned up. When maintaining cover as an Imperial offier, Blastwell used a TIE Interceptor to transport himself to the ''Garrett''. ==Behind the scenes== Hamo Blastwell was created by [[Rusel DeMaria]] for ''[[The Farlander Papers]]'', the short novella which told the story of Keyan Farlander, the player character in the [[1993]] [[LucasArts]] video game [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]. Both that story and the game were released [[February]] of the year.{{LucasArts|url=20th/history_2.htm|text=20th Anniversary History, Part Two: The Classics, 1990 - 1994}} In [[June]] of the same year,{{CargoBay|url=item-detail/46|text=X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide}} ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' mentioned Blastwell and gave the impression that he was killed at the Battle of Yavin. ''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'', published that same year. However, in ''[[TIE Fighter: The Official Strategy Guide]]'', released in [[1994]], he was established to have survived and subsequently went undercover in the Imperial Navy. ==Appearances== *{{StoryCite|story=The Farlander Papers|book=Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)}} {{1st|[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]}} *[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] {{C|Starfighter only}} ==Sources== *''[[TIE Fighter: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' *''[[TIE Fighter Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Blastwell, Hamo}} [[Category:Blue Squadron personnel]] [[Category:Gold Squadron personnel]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Navy pilots of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Red Squadron personnel]] [[Category:Spies]]
